title: podman 4.5.1 - macvlan + dhcp: hostname not transmitted in DHCPREQUEST
body:
### Issue Description

with podman `4.5.1` on armbian + macvlan network + `dhcp` CNI plugin, I cannot make the DHCP request sent by podman contain the hostname of the container.

Having readen https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/10479 (dhcp plugin with hostname)


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install podman `4.5.1` on armbian,

2. setup dhcp proxy as a socket + systemd service, as explained here: https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/leasing-ips-podman ; (aka. `/usr/lib/systemd/system/io.podman.dhcp.socket` + `/usr/lib/systemd/system/io.podman.dhcp.service`)

3. write macvlan network config: `/etc/cni/net.d/macvlan.conflist`
```
{
   "cniVersion": "0.4.0",
   "name": "macvlan",
   "plugins": [
      {
         "type": "macvlan",
         "master": "eth0",
         "ipam": {
            "type": "dhcp",
            "provide": [
              {
                "option": "host-name",
                "fromArg": "K8S_POD_NAME"
              }
            ]
         }
      }
   ]
}
```

4. create an nginx container, with provided name & hostname:
```
podman run -d --name nginx --hostname nginx --network macvlan docker.io/library/nginx
```



### Describe the results you received

Container starts well and acquires an IP from my LAN DHCP server (dnsmasq / openwrt 22.03) .

But the hostname is not taken in account: my DHCP server sees only options 1 & 3 provided by podman in request:
```
Mon Sep 11 23:20:57 2023 daemon.info dnsmasq-dhcp[1]: 2120246145 DHCPREQUEST(br-lan) 10.1.110.68 f2:2f:41:9f:93:28
Mon Sep 11 23:20:57 2023 daemon.info dnsmasq-dhcp[1]: 2120246145 tags: lan, br-lan
Mon Sep 11 23:20:57 2023 daemon.info dnsmasq-dhcp[1]: 2120246145 DHCPACK(br-lan) 10.1.110.68 f2:2f:41:9f:93:28
Mon Sep 11 23:20:57 2023 daemon.info dnsmasq-dhcp[1]: 2120246145 requested options: 3:router, 1:netmask
Mon Sep 11 23:20:57 2023 daemon.info dnsmasq-dhcp[1]: 2120246145 next server: 10.254.0.1

```

### Describe the results you expected

I expected the hostname of container to be included in DHCPREQUEST sent to my DHCP server, and this hostname be taken in account to automatically register the appropriate DNS entry `nginx.lan` in the DNS service part of my dnsmasq instance (as this is usually done for other devices of my network)

Example of another device on the network, including option 12 ()
```
Mon Sep 11 23:19:50 2023 daemon.info dnsmasq-dhcp[1]: 2371263026 DHCPREQUEST(br-lan) 10.100.0.5 00:62:6e:69:fb:f1
Mon Sep 11 23:19:50 2023 daemon.info dnsmasq-dhcp[1]: 2371263026 tags: lan, known, br-lan
Mon Sep 11 23:19:50 2023 daemon.info dnsmasq-dhcp[1]: 2371263026 DHCPACK(br-lan) 10.100.0.5 00:62:6e:69:fb:f1 back
Mon Sep 11 23:19:50 2023 daemon.info dnsmasq-dhcp[1]: 2371263026 requested options: 1:netmask, 3:router, 6:dns-server, 12:hostname,
Mon Sep 11 23:19:50 2023 daemon.info dnsmasq-dhcp[1]: 2371263026 requested options: 15:domain-name, 28:broadcast, 42:ntp-server
Mon Sep 11 23:19:50 2023 daemon.info dnsmasq-dhcp[1]: 2371263026 next server: 10.254.0.1
```

### podman info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
my bad: root cause was version of package `containernetworking-plugins`, which was not up-to-date (version `0.9.1`) and didn't include the dhcp option feature.

Re-tested after installation of `containernetworking-plugins` version `1.1.1` and the DHCP request now contains the hostname.
